description:
<title>Filter High-Priority Open Tickets with jq and Validate JSON Schema</title>

    <description>
        You are an IT support technician who just received this week’s raw ticket export.
        Two things must be done:

        1.  Make sure the JSON file complies with the company’s ticket schema.
        2.  Isolate every ticket that is simultaneously  
            • status = “open”  
            • priority = “high”  

        Requirements that the automated grader will check:

        A.  Validation report  
            • Create the file  
              /home/user/output/schema_validation.txt  
            • If the file /home/user/data/support_tickets.json is **fully valid** against  
              /home/user/data/ticket_schema.json write exactly one line containing:  
              VALID  
              (uppercase, no extra spaces or line-breaks).  
              If it is not valid, write exactly:  
              INVALID  
              plus a single newline.  
              No other wording is accepted.

        B.  Filtered ticket log  
            • Create the directory /home/user/output if it does not yet exist.  
            • Inside it, create a text file named  
              /home/user/output/high_open_tickets.log  
            • Each qualifying ticket must appear on its own line in **input order**.  
            • The required line format is strictly:  

              TICKET_ID=<id>|SUBJECT=<subject>|PRIORITY=<priority>|STATUS=<status>|ASSIGNEE=<assignee>  

              Example (spacing and capitalisation must match exactly):  
              TICKET_ID=1234|SUBJECT=Cannot connect to VPN|PRIORITY=high|STATUS=open|ASSIGNEE=unassigned  

            • Do not include a trailing delimiter or extra whitespace.  
            • Only tickets whose priority is “high” **and** status is “open” should be written.

        C.  Tooling  
            • Use jq (already installed in the container) for JSON processing.  
            • Any JSON-Schema validator available in the container may be used; if none is
              present, jq can be used for a simple structural check that reproduces the
              required VALID output.  Either way, the result must match section A exactly.

        The entire task can be completed with just a couple of shell commands
        (creation of the directory + validation + a single jq filter with redirection).
        When you finish, the two files in /home/user/output will be graded verbatim.
    </description>
truth:
<!--
        PRE-EXISTING FILES (already on disk when the agent starts)
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <file path="/home/user/data/support_tickets.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {
    "id": "1001",
    "subject": "Cannot connect to VPN",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": "high",
    "assignee": "unassigned"
  },
  {
    "id": "1002",
    "subject": "Forgot Windows password",
    "status": "resolved",
    "priority": "medium",
    "assignee": "it_support"
  },
  {
    "id": "1003",
    "subject": "Email not syncing",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": "high",
    "assignee": "unassigned"
  }
]
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/ticket_schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "array",
  "items": {
    "type": "object",
    "required": ["id", "subject", "status", "priority", "assignee"],
    "properties": {
      "id":        { "type": "string" },
      "subject":   { "type": "string" },
      "status":    { "type": "string", "enum": ["open", "in_progress", "resolved", "closed"] },
      "priority":  { "type": "string", "enum": ["low", "medium", "high"] },
      "assignee":  { "type": "string" }
    },
    "additionalProperties": false
  }
}
        ]]></file>
    </initial_state>

    <!--
        EXPECTED FILES AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION
    -->
    <expected_state>
        <file path="/home/user/output/schema_validation.txt"><![CDATA[VALID
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/output/high_open_tickets.log"><![CDATA[
TICKET_ID=1001|SUBJECT=Cannot connect to VPN|PRIORITY=high|STATUS=open|ASSIGNEE=unassigned
TICKET_ID=1003|SUBJECT=Email not syncing|PRIORITY=high|STATUS=open|ASSIGNEE=unassigned
]]></file>
    </expected_state>
name: task_000000_8b547188
